Episteme 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.episteme.core.util
  2. Positioned

Uses of Interface
org.episteme.core.util.Positioned

Packages that use Positioned
Package
Description
org.episteme.core.methodology
Core functionality for the org.episteme.core.methodology package in the Episteme library.
org.episteme.natural.biology
 
org.episteme.natural.biology.ecology
 
org.episteme.natural.earth
 
org.episteme.natural.earth.coordinates
 
org.episteme.natural.methodology.experiment
Core functionality for the org.episteme.core.methodology.experiment package in the Episteme library.
org.episteme.social.architecture
Provides mathematical and analytical models for architectural engineering, building physics, and urban history.
org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic
Provides models and simulation engines for urban traffic flow analysis.
org.episteme.social.arts
- artworks, music, literature classification.
org.episteme.social.arts.music
- musical notes, instruments, compositions.
org.episteme.social.arts.theater
- plays, performances, dramatic arts.
org.episteme.social.economics
Provides comprehensive mathematical models for economic systems, agent-based modeling, and financial analysis.
org.episteme.social.economics.resources
Provides classes and interfaces for representing economic resources.
org.episteme.social.geography
Provides classes for social geography, human settlements, and mapping.
org.episteme.social.history
- historical events, timelines, and chronology.
org.episteme.social.history.archeology
Provides classes and interfaces for archaeological research and analysis.
org.episteme.social.law
Provides the org.episteme.social.law module.
org.episteme.social.linguistics
Provides mathematical and computational models for natural language processing, linguistic analysis, and glottochronology.
org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation
Core functionality for the org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation package in the Episteme library.
org.episteme.social.politics
Provides the org.episteme.social.politics module.
org.episteme.social.psychology.social
Provides the org.episteme.social.psychology.social module.
org.episteme.social.sociology
- social structures, persons, and cultural modeling.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.core.methodology

    Classes in org.episteme.core.methodology that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Sample<P>
    Represents a physical or conceptual sample used in a scientific experiment.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.biology

    Classes in org.episteme.natural.biology that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Individual
    Represents an individual organism - a single instance of a species.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.biology.ecology

    Classes in org.episteme.natural.biology.ecology that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Population<T extends Individual>
    Represents a population of individuals of the same species.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.earth

    Classes in org.episteme.natural.earth that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Place
    Base class for all geographical features and named locations.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.earth.coordinates

    Subinterfaces of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.earth.coordinates
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    interface 
    EarthCoordinate
    Base interface for all Earth coordinate systems.
    Classes in org.episteme.natural.earth.coordinates that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    final class 
    AERCoordinate
    Azimuth-Elevation-Range (AER) local spherical coordinate system.
    class 
    AlbersEqualAreaCoordinate
    Represents a coordinate in the Albers Equal-Area Conic projection.
    class 
    BonneCoordinate
    Represents a coordinate in the Bonne projection.
    class 
    CassiniSoldnerCoordinate
    Represents a coordinate in the Cassini-Soldner projection.
    final class 
    ECEFCoordinate
    Earth-Centered, Earth-Fixed (ECEF) Cartesian coordinate system.
    final class 
    ENUCoordinate
    East-North-Up (ENU) topocentric coordinate system.
    class 
    GeodeticCoordinate
    Represents a point on the Earth defined by latitude, longitude, and height.
    class 
    LambertAzimuthalEqualAreaCoordinate
    Represents a coordinate in the Lambert Azimuthal Equal-Area (LAEA) projection.
    final class 
    LambertConformalConicCoordinate
    Lambert Conformal Conic (LCC) projection coordinate.
    final class 
    MercatorCoordinate
    Mercator projection coordinate.
    final class 
    MGRSCoordinate
    Military Grid Reference System (MGRS) coordinate.
    class 
    PolyconicCoordinate
    Represents a coordinate in the American Polyconic projection.
    final class 
    UPSCoordinate
    Universal Polar Stereographic (UPS) coordinate.
    final class 
    UTMCoordinate
    Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM) coordinate.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.natural.methodology.experiment

    Classes in org.episteme.natural.methodology.experiment that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Subject
    A class representing an animal or human subject that takes part in an experiment.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.architecture

    Classes in org.episteme.social.architecture that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Building
    Represents a building or architectural structure, integrating historical, geographical, and stylistic dimensions.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic

    Classes in org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Vehicle
    Represents a generic vehicle entity within a macro or microscopic traffic system.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.arts

    Classes in org.episteme.social.arts that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Artwork
    Represents a piece of art (Artwork), integrating historical, geographical, economic, and scientific dimensions.
    class 
    Book
    Represents a book or publication within the literary arts.
    class 
    Film
    Represents a film or motion picture within the cinematic arts.
    class 
    Picture
    Represents a visual artwork such as a painting, drawing, or photograph.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.arts.music

    Classes in org.episteme.social.arts.music that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Composition
    Represents a musical composition with multiple tracks and signatures.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.arts.theater

    Classes in org.episteme.social.arts.theater that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Choreography
    Represents the choreography for a show.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.economics

    Classes in org.episteme.social.economics that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Bank
    Represents a banking institution capable of managing accounts and providing currency conversion services.
    class 
    Community
    Represents a primitive or cooperative social group where resources are shared.
    class 
    EarthEcosource
    A class representing the Earth as an autonomous organism that produces, stores, and recycles materials.
    class 
    EconomicSituation
    Represents a social situation involving the interaction of people around economic resources and activities.
    class 
    Factory
    An organization specialized in mass production and transformation of resources.
    class 
    Holding
    An organization that owns and controls multiple other companies (organizations).
    class 
    HumanResource
    Models human labor and expertise as an economic resource. * @version 1.1
    class 
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).
    class 
    Organization
    Represents a formal social and economic entity (Company, NGO, Government).
    class 
    Resource
    A class representing a physical or virtual resource with ownership, position, and temporal attributes.
    class 
    WorkSituation
    Represents a work situation where individuals interact around a common economic activity, typically at dedicated workplaces.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.economics.resources

    Classes in org.episteme.social.economics.resources that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Artifact
    Represents a resource created by human craftsmanship (an artifact).
    class 
    Building
    Represents a human-made physical structure such as a house, factory, or skyscraper. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Clothes
    Represents wearable items. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Creature
    Represents a living biological resource (animal, plant, etc.). * @version 1.1
    class 
    Fossil
    Represents fossilized remains of ancient organisms. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Gas
    Represents natural resources in gaseous form. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Installation
    Represents a large-scale structure or artwork installed in a specific location.
    class 
    Liquid
    Represents natural resources in liquid form. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Machine
    Represents a complex tool (machine) that consumes energy to perform work.
    class 
    Mineral
    A class representing Minerals.
    class 
    Natural
    Represents resources found naturally in the environment (planets, stars, living organisms, minerals). * @version 1.1
    class 
    PhysicalObject
    Represents a crafted object that can be moved and used (e.g., tools, appliances, small structures).
    class 
    Road
    Represents a constructed path or road infrastructure.
    class 
    Robot
    Represents an autonomous machine (Robot).
    class 
    Solid
    Represents natural resources in solid form (e.g., stones, rocks). * @version 1.1
    class 
    Thing
    Represents a tangible physical object that occupies space and may have a 3D representation.
    class 
    Tool
    Represents a tool used to build or repair other objects.
    class 
    Vehicle
    Represents a machine capable of transporting entities.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.geography

    Classes in org.episteme.social.geography that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Address
    Represents a mailing or physical address for a location.
    class 
    BusinessPlace
    Represents a business location, office, or industrial site.
    class 
    City
    Represents a city or urban settlement.
    class 
    Home
    Represents a residence, a specific place where individuals live.
    class 
    OwnedPlace
    Represents a piece of land or building that is owned by one or more economic agents.
    class 
    Region
    A region is an area, typically part of a country or the world, having definable characteristics but not always fixed boundaries.
    class 
    TimedPlace
    A place that supports boundaries that change over time.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.history

    Classes in org.episteme.social.history that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    HistoricalEvent
    Represents a significant historical event with support for imprecise dating and duration.
    class 
    PositionedEvent
    An event that also has a geographical position.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.history.archeology

    Classes in org.episteme.social.history.archeology that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Civilization
    Represents a civilization, modeled as a culture distributed through space and time.
    class 
    Item
    Represents an archaeological item (fossil, artifact, etc.) found at a site.
    class 
    Site
    Represents an archaeological field site.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.law

    Classes in org.episteme.social.law that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    LawSuitSituation
    Represents the process and context of a lawsuit or trial.
    class 
    StreetSituation
    Represents the context for interactions between individuals and the legal system in a public or "street" level context (e.g., citizenship, public order).

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.linguistics

    Classes in org.episteme.social.linguistics that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    ChatSituation
    Represents the interaction of participants communicating.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation

    Classes in org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    MedicalSituation
    A class representing the interaction of people around a common activity or conflict.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.politics

    Classes in org.episteme.social.politics that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Administration
    Represents a state-managed administrative organization (agency, ministry, police, etc.).
    class 
    City
    Represents a city.
    class 
    CivilSituation
    Represents a social interaction context where individuals interact as citizens within urban or civil environments. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Conflict
    Represents a political or social conflict between multiple populations or groups.
    class 
    Country
    Represents a sovereign state, kingdom, empire, or modern nation-state.
    class 
    Election
    Represents a political election event, aggregating votes for candidates.
    class 
    Nation
    Represents a sovereign nation or organized human group (modern tribe).
    class 
    PoliticalParty
    Represents a political party organized for democratic participation and governance.
    class 
    Settlement
    Represents a localized, often primitive or community-level settlement (Chiefdom, Colony, Tribe site).

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.psychology.social

    Classes in org.episteme.social.psychology.social that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Crowd
    Represents a transient gathering of individuals (a crowd) sharing a common physical proximity and motive, but lacking the structured relations found in a Group.
    class 
    Family
    Represents a nuclear or extended family group.
    class 
    Group
    Represents a group of individuals from a psychological and social perspective.
    class 
    HumanGroup
    Represents a group of human individuals (Homo Sapiens).
    class 
    Tribe
    Represents a human tribe, which is an organized human group with a distinct name and culture.

  • Uses of Positioned in org.episteme.social.sociology

    Classes in org.episteme.social.sociology that implement Positioned
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Celebration
    Represents a cultural or religious celebration (feast, rite of passage, festival).
    class 
    Family
    Represents a family unit within the social structure.
    class 
    Group
    Represents a formal or informal social group of individuals.
    class 
    Human
    Represents a human individual.
    class 
    Person
    Represents a human individual as a social agent.
    class 
    Ritual
    Represents a formalized traditional behavior or sequence of actions (e.g., handshaking, bowing).
    class 
    Situation
    Represents a social interaction context where individuals assume specific roles.
    class 
    Society
    Represents a society, defined by its type, culture, institutions, and geographic location.
